title: Complex Numbers
summary: This page covers properties of complex numbers including extracting the imaginary part via conjugate subtraction, the product of a complex number with its conjugate yielding the squared magnitude, and the geometric interpretation of complex exponentials in the complex plane. Key identities are derived for -1, 1, j, and -j in polar exponential form, noting that angles are defined only up to integer multiples of 2π.

figures.caption: Understanding some useful identities in terms of re^(jθ).
